programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A man holds an antenatal class hostage, and the expectant mothers all start going into labour. Fortunately, Dr Sloan is on hand to resolve matters. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble risks his own life to save an employer from committing a crime

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Returning home from Europe a year after her son was killed, a woman is charged with murdering the man she accused of being responsible for his death

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part one of two. While on a fishing trip, a drug smuggling operation is uncovered. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A model is electrocuted at a fashion show, and the CSIs quickly determine her death was no accident. Investigating further, they discover the case is connected to a heroin-smuggling ring involving a fashion designer - and a prisoner who once held Horatio's son Kyle hostage. Crime drama, starring David Caruso

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ducky works on a case involving his estranged childhood best friend, travelling to London with Special Agent Bishop to interview his pal's family and co-workers. While away, the detective reflects on his own past choices, and examines how they have affected his present life. Alice Krige guest stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: The Leonettis, a couple from Kimble's past who hold him responsible for the death of their child, see him working as a hospital orderly and notify the police

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry deals with a complicated case of corporate embezzlement and murder when a secretary involved in a million-dollar swindle is found dead

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Kidnappers strike on Halloween, wounding a marine sergeant and snatching his daughter - leaving the team members wondering why anyone would try to extort money from someone on such a modest salary. As the agents begin searching for a motive, Abby surprises everyone by turning up at the lab in a revealing fancy-dress costume

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A crooked police officer tries to kill Alex after he receives insider information about a ring of Dallas lawmen stealing confiscated drugs. Chuck Norris and Larry Sellers star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When a mutilated body is found in a car at a scrapyard, the NCIS agents find themselves trying to locate a serial killer. Meanwhile, Abby and McGee head to Mexico and investigate the 20-year-old mystery of a drug dealer's murder. Their actions attract the attention of the head of a local drug cartel, and links begin to emerge between their case, the serial killings and Gibbs's past

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team tries to find the culprit behind an ambush on a vehicle transporting marines in the desert, which resulted in the death of one man and abduction of two others. Kensi and Deeks bond as they track the SUV that took the missing soldiers, and Sam risks using a compromised cover identity to meet a former contact in a bid to discover if drug cartels were involved. Meanwhile, Nate returns from his mystery assignment

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team joins forces with military investigators to ascertain whether terrorists were responsible for an explosion at a golf course in which a marine colonel was killed. programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the murder of his old friend Mayor Ridgeway. The motive appears political, but things are not as clear-cut as they seem. Crime drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jack gets closer to a conman friend who is trying to straighten out his life, but a fatal car crash complicates matters and an investigation ensues. The case leads to a luxury health spa, where Dr Sloan seizes the opportunity to operate undercover

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A prison doctor is held hostage by one of Sloan's patients - a convicted criminal demanding to see the newscaster who covered his trial. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is hired by the Department of Defense to break into Fort Knox to test their security - but when a sinister discovery is made, the group is forced to find a way into the complex again. Drama inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is employed to install a concrete sarcophagus at the site of the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant before further radiation leaks occur

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators examine the death of US marine Tommy Hill, who appears to have been fatally stabbed en route to a homecoming party thrown in his honour by his foster family. McGee and Ziva follow a trail of blood that leads them to a mobile phone, while Abby discovers the victim had been trying to hack into Virginia's state website and Ducky's autopsy reveals that Hill had been injected with a sedative

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A man who died after being knocked down by a van is revealed to be a professional hitman responsible for at least six contract killings - and his next target was to be a navy lieutenant commander. Abby is delighted at being a match for a teenager who needs a kidney transplant, but she is informed that someone else with very similar DNA has also been identified as suitable. Has she found a relative she never knew about?

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a coastguard officer on board an abandoned cargo ship draws the team's attention to a family of Lebanese refugees hoping to find a new home in America. Meanwhile, Tony and McGee try to find the perfect woman for Gibbs. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a Navy lieutenant is found on the side of a remote road and toxicology tests reveal he died of overhydration. When another man is found dead nearby, the NCIS team begins to suspect a serial killer is on the loose. Meanwhile, Ducky introduces Gibbs to his new girlfriend. Crime drama, guest starring Cheryl Ladd

Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Return of the American drama following the work of the Naval Criminal Investigative Service. Gibbs and McGee travel to Russia to escort a computer expert connected to classified intel. However, the mission is compromised and they are forced to go off the grid while being hunted by Russian mercenaries. Meanwhile, the team in DC frantically tries to locate the missing pair. Mark Harmon and Sean Murray star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: First of an eight-part story. Smithy helps the victim of a knife attack on the Jasmine Allen estate and renews acquaintance with Carly Samuels, a girl he had been helping, who witnessed the incident. It soon emerges that Carly is afraid of her violent boyfriend and keen to start a new life miles away from London. Meanwhile, Beth and Nate encounter a young carer who seeks relief from his responsibilities by setting fire to cars

programme.desc: Ric resigns from the board, and tells Annalese to stay with Michael. programme.desc: A gang of racists uses fireworks to attack an Asian family. One of the thugs is burned and admitted to hospital, where Guppy bravely stands up for himself when he becomes the target of racial abuse. Mr O'Brien demands to know why Josh hit his son, and Luke warns Woody not to let Susan take advantage of his generosity. Medical drama, guest starring Kika Mirylees, Shobu Kapoor and Jodi Albert

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of eight. Smithy battles in vain to save Carly's life following the shooting and struggles with his feelings back at the station when he is tasked as the family liaison officer. A mobile unit is set up on the estate to help with community problems in light of the gang violence, and Beth's decision to bring in Harry the dog proves a winner with Nate when he realises how popular he is with the girls. Cleveland Campbell guest stars

programme.desc: A forgetful clock-mender contacts the sleuth when he becomes the target of a couple of small-time crooks. Armed with advice from DCI Adams, Hetty starts her investigation at a video store and soon discovers that there is more to the case than meets the eye

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Police drama, starring John Simm. Sam experiences visions that seem to suggest a return to the 21st century, but wakes to find himself still stuck in 1973. His mood lifts when he comes face to face with the younger version of a killer he arrested in 2006 and realises he has the chance to prevent a crime before it happens - but his unorthodox methods surprise colleagues at the station. With Philip Glenister and Marc Warren

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reopens the 16-year-old case of an infamous getaway driver convicted of murdering his wife when new evidence suggests it could have been a miscarriage of justice. But Gerry, who was on the original investigation, refuses to believe the prisoner is anything but guilty. Meanwhile, Brian is in a reflective mood, convinced his days at Ucos are numbered after his actions against former police commander Embleton. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star as the veteran coppers, with Amanda Redman as their boss

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Harry and Nikki investigate a shooting at the Dutch embassy, where several people are killed and the ambassador's daughter Klara is wounded. Complications arise when orders are given to keep the identity of a dead bodyguard a secret, and an outside pathologist is appointed to explore the case. As Klara recovers in hospital, she gradually begins to remember who was responsible for the incident. Kate Ashfield (Collision), Lorcan Cranitch (Cracker) and Matthew McNulty (Lark Rise to Candleford) guest star

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: A humiliated and jealous Evelyn exacts revenge by spreading the sordid secrets of Karen's past around Skelthwaite and patients soon refuse to be treated by her, leaving it up to Anna to discover why a teenage epileptic continues to suffer attacks despite his medication. Simon struggles to cope with the preparations for his forthcoming wedding and proves easy prey for Evelyn, who seems intent on wreaking as much havoc as she can

programme.desc: Despatched by Munro to kill a French colonel, Sharpe is wounded in battle and hovers at death's door - but Harper and a mute nun nurse him back to health. Once recovered, he sets out in search of vengeance with a newly forged sword. Period adventure, starring Sean Bean and Daragh O'Malley

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon agrees to play along with her wayward husband's hare-brained scheme to pay off his gambling debts, and soon gets cajoled into appearing on a TV game show and trying to track down a collectable car in Berlin. Meanwhile, Dorien leaves Marcus and moves into the girls' house next door. The 1990 Christmas special, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson, Lesley Joseph and Peter Polycarpou

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: First episode of John Sullivan's comedy series, following the lives of Only Fools and Horses characters Boycie and Marlene. Denzil tells Boycie the infamous Driscoll Brothers are after the supergrass who had them jailed - prompting the car salesman to sell his business and move to rural Shropshire. John Challis and Sue Holderness star, with a guest appearance by Paul Barber

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Tyler and Marlene are not impressed by their new surroundings and want a swift return to Peckham, but Boycie warms to the idea of becoming a 'gentleman farmer', which seemingly involves doing little work and mingling with the country set. His manager Elgin persuades him to retain all his staff

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie puts Marlene under pressure to fit in with the locals, so she decides to take up cookery - an idea that does not go down well with her family. She orders her husband to pick blackberries for a pie, causing him to inadvertently trespass on the land of his anti-English neighbour. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: An Inspector Calls
programme.desc: Adaptation of JB Priestley's stage play. In 1912, a detective (David Thewlis) arrives in a town where a young woman has committed suicide. Interrupting a wealthy family in the midst of a lavish party, he unravels the events that led to the woman's death - an investigation with devastating repercussions as secrets are exposed and each member of the family realises they have all unwittingly played a part. Also starring Miranda Richardson and Ken Stott

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Philip Henderson is found dead in his field following an altercation the previous day with his neighbour Doug Randall about his failed crop. Suspicion falls on the neighbour and his new wife, who was known to have had ongoing problems with Philip over his unpaid bar tab at her pub. However, as Mike and the team dig further into the mystery more suspects come into play. Neill Rea stars

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When Kembleford's new cricket captain is framed for murder, Father Brown must prove his innocence to give his team any chance of beating neighbouring village Hambleston. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: WPC 56
programme.desc: Sergeant Fenton discovers a local forger making a fake passport for Lenny Powell, and gets a shock when Chris Hutton turns up at the handover. A fight between Fenton and Lenny ensues, and the latter admits he beat up the boxer, but denies killing Rosie or Daniel Pembrook. Gina's world is rocked when the murderer reveals himself, and she has to make a life-or-death decision. Police drama set in the 1950s, starring Charlie De'Ath and Michael Higgs

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: A satanist is murdered on Halloween. Jim (Mark McManus) is assigned the investigation, considered too tough for his beleaguered colleagues, and soon detects a subtle whiff of brimstone hanging over the entire case. James MacPherson and Blythe Duff also star

programme.desc: Jane begins to wonder about the chemistry between herself and Rochester, who disappoints her when he leaves without warning. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar readies itself for Christmas 1960 with a festive bus trip to see the Regent Street lights, while a visit from a BBC film crew promises to make it a festive holiday to remember. However, it's a difficult time for grieving mother Iris, bringing back memories of her long-departed baby daughter. At Nonnatus, things are tense as Sister Monica Joan determines to bring the Christmas spirit early - despite Sister Evangelina's disgust. As the community prepares for a televised carol concert, it becomes apparent that Sister Monica Joan has gone missing. Jenny Agutter and Judy Parfitt star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Easter approaches in the spring of 1961, and Patsy helps to deliver a baby that is born with severe deformities. programme.title: Morning Live

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A small flat in Tooting, south London, a huge former care home near a large park in Liverpool and a fire-damaged house in Silverdale in Shropshire are all sold at auction. Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear their plans

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley present appeals for help with unsolved cases. Reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers discover an illegal puppy farm in Cannock, putting profit over care. Thirty dogs and two racoons are found kept in filthy conditions, some of them locked in old cars. There's no water, food or in some cases ventilation. Plus, a rogue kitchen trader swindling customers out of thousands of pounds

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, as they hunt for potentially profitable items to take to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also looks at the art and history of Persian carpets

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: When hearing-impaired Rosie is told her deafness is getting worse, she is desperate to learn sign language. However, she faces opposition from husband Nathan. Written by Charlie Swinbourne, with Sophie Stone and Kelvin Fletcher

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to create a piece for the home of a buyer who has returned to her family roots in Shropshire following a health scare

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah heads to Yorkshire to help a couple of retired teachers with a £450,000 budget find a country home and finally return to their family roots. Along the way, Sonali meets a young Dales farmer who is using his dairy herd to produce Icelandic-inspired Skyr yoghurt

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Health professionals Hayley and Sunni compete. The pair met on social media over their shared love of a plant-based diet, so in an all-vegan show contest are keen to take the ingredients they cook with all the time and elevate them to new heights. Paired up with chef Romy Gill, Hayley is keen to rustle up some mouth-watering vegan treats in the Green Pepper kitchen. In the Red Tomato kitchen, Sunni is paired with chef Jeremy Pang, who brings his own flair and style to Sunni's usual ingredients. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an Edwardian shoe closet, a bronze racehorse, a 17th-century writing desk and Concorde service medals. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Great Britain hurdling great Colin Jackson, former England striker Darren Bent, ex-WBA Super Middleweight champion boxer George Groves and Commonwealth Games gold medal-winning swimmer Aimee Willmott join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The second quarter-final sees four talented heat-winners return to vie for a place in knockout week by participating in the Critic's Test. The challenge - set by William Sitwell, one of the UK's toughest food critics - is to cook a dish inspired by a holiday. Gregg Wallace and John Torode preside as the contenders endeavour to take another step closer to being crowned MasterChef Champion 2021

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bailey is triumphant as she takes part in the first round of the school science competition - but her joy doesn't last when she returns home to bad news. Lucas is interviewed for a job by Caleb, who isn't fooled by the ex-convict's attempts to pose under a different name - but insists he turn up on Monday or else

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Under the eye of the acerbic chef, contestants Paul and Stu and couple Sophie and Claud answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board - the more bars they build up, the more they can win up to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by comic actress Amy Poehler, star and director of the rebellious new comedy Moxie and comedian Rob Beckett, who is promoting his podcast Lockdown Parenting Hell. Call the Midwife's Leonie Elliott also guests, alongside Hollywood star Jennifer Garner and singer-songwriter Nick Jonas. With music from singer-songwriter Tom Odell

programme.title: Fern Brady: Power & Chaos
programme.desc: In front of a packed crowd in her native Scotland, the rising star delivers an energetic stand-up performance tackling issues of sexuality, feminism, power, Brexit, Britishness, Scottishness and nationality, all with her usual caustic wit and electric stage craft

programme.title: Mum's List (2016)
programme.desc: Young couple Kate and Singe's lives are turned upside down when Kate is diagnosed with an incurable breast cancer. Over the course of her final few months, she creates a list - writing her thoughts and memories down to help the man she loved create the best life possible for their two sons after she is gone. Drama based on the book by St John Greene, starring Rafe Spall, Emilia Fox and Elaine Cassidy

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Theo Randall and Vivek Singh, while guest Stacey Dooley faces her food heaven or hell. Plus, archive clips and wine choices with Olly Smith

programme.title: Recipes That Made Me
programme.desc: Nisha Katona travels to London to discover more about food from the tropical island of Sri Lanka, and meets Sri Lankans who are introducing their authentic recipes from home, including a group serving traditional dishes in a a lakeside cafe in London's Victoria Park. Nisha also visits the world food market in St Katharine's Dock to try a much-loved Sri Lankan street food dish

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Fulham v Manchester City and Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents as the hunt for bargains heads to Oswestry, Shropshire, where experts Jonathan Pratt and John Cameron assist two teams of friends competing to buy three items in the hope of making the most profit at auction. Natasha also heads to Hawarden, Flintstone, to visit Gladstone's Library, Britain's only prime ministerial library, where she reveals what a wheelbarrow and a former prime minister have in common

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore's recycling mission takes her to Bredbury, Greater Manchester, where she finds a haul of pine doors, old rope and glass demijohns to convert into new, exciting objects

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Jo and Millie from Berwick-upon-Tweed run a food waste cafe. Today they are looking for new ways to use the food that is most often donated. Ellis Barrie and Romy Gill come up with a couple of ideas, while Rylan Clark-Neal keeps things ticking over

programme.title: Attenborough's Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the extraordinary ways that some animals use colour to hide and disappear into the background. The Bengal tigers of central India use their striped patterns to dazzle colour blind prey, while zebras on the Masai Mara use motion dazzle to confuse predators. Other animals use colour to trick and to deceive. On Australia's Great Barrier Reef, a blue-striped blenny uses colours to mimic other fish and launch a sudden attack

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo and his son Luci compete in this special edition of the game show, stacking the gold bars on the balance board in a bid to win up to £100,000 for charity. But if they have answered incorrectly, the bars they stack will amount to nothing - and only serve to add further weight to the already precarious board, which could collapse at any second and leave them with nothing

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Married couple Sam and Louise from Aberdeen take on The Wall, hoping to combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Will they get the questions right? And can they walk away with cash to buy a bigger house for when they can start a family? Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a star-studded version of the general knowledge quiz. Taking part are Gok Wan, Brian Conley, Richard McCourt, Dominic Wood, Libby Clegg, Stef Reid, Stephen K Amos and Zoe Lyons

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Faith realises her marriage is unsalvageable and falls into Dylan's arms, while Fenisha struggles with admitting her horrifying fears about Bodhi. Connie shows an eager Grace some of the trauma equipment that is used in the ED, and Grace is inspired by her mother's profession. Connie works a hectic shift and feels guilty for neglecting her daughter yet again, but the youngster is unphased and understanding, buoyed by a newfound interest in medicine

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: AC-12's investigation exposes a vicious criminal gang run from within the police, and Dryden is charged with conspiracy to pervert the course of justice. While Arnott now appears convinced of Denton's innocence, Fleming remains determined to prove she had a role in the ambush. Crime thriller, starring Keeley Hawes, Martin Compston and Vicky McClure

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Fulham v Manchester City at Craven Cottage and Leeds United v Chelsea at Elland Road. The Citizens will have been expecting to maintain their excellent form at the expense of struggling Fulham, while the Blues will have hoped to emulate their 3-1 win over Leeds at Stamford Bridge in December. Plus, Crystal Palace v West Bromwich Albion and Everton v Burnley

programme.title: Eagle Eye (2008)
programme.desc: Two strangers find themselves on the run from the authorities when they receive mysterious phone calls informing them their lives are in danger. They are forced to follow the instructions of a woman who is tracking their every move, while at the same time trying to discover who is behind the conspiracy. Thriller, starring Shia LaBeouf, Michelle Monaghan, Rosario Dawson and Michael Chiklis

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Ben Thompson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Fulham v Manchester City at Craven Cottage and Leeds United v Chelsea at Elland Road. The Citizens will have been expecting to maintain their excellent form at the expense of struggling Fulham, while the Blues will have hoped to emulate their 3-1 win over Leeds at Stamford Bridge in December. Plus, Crystal Palace v West Bromwich Albion and Everton v Burnley

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the studio audience

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley introduces a church service to celebrate Mothering Sunday from St Cuthbert's Church in Preston, led by the Rev Mark Bradford

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Southampton v Brighton & Hove Albion (Kick-off 12.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the Premier League encounter at St Mary's Stadium. Southampton are looking to complete a double over the Seagulls, having come back from behind to prevail 2-1 when the sides met at Amex Stadium in December thanks to goals by Jannik Vestergaard and Danny Ings, but Brighton have generally been slightly stronger on their travels this term. With analysis from Dion Dublin

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Ireland (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the match from the fourth round of fixtures, held at BT Murrayfield. Ireland recorded a 19-12 victory when the sides met at Aviva Stadium in last season's competition, with Jonathan Sexton scoring the only try of the game kicking his side's other 14 points, and they also prevailed 22-13 here in 2019, but the Scots have produced some strong recent performances and will be hopeful of revenge. Gabby Logan presents, with analysis from John Barclay, Martin Johnson and Andy Nicol, and commentary from Eddie Butler, Chris Paterson and Jamie Heaslip

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison joins Adam Henson and his team for 48 hours of hectic lambing. She may be the rookie in Adam's lambing shed, but Ellie quickly gets to grips with multiple births, touch-and-go adoptions, and the emotions of the life-or-death decisions faced every day during this busiest of spring seasons on the farm. Tom Heap is on the case to find out why mountains of wool, historically the nation's most valuable sheep product, are now piling up in warehouses across the country

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Forty Hall in London. Treasures include a pair of hands crafted by Lucian Freud, a destination board from the front of an early London bus, and a model of Donald Campbell's Bluebird, which broke the land-speed record in 1964. There is also a rare set of wedding jewellery from Nepal, and a diamond cross and gold necklace with a hefty price tag

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to the Lake District for an emotional reunion with their own dads' old cars. Elsewhere, Chris heads out on track to test Lamborghini's most powerful car ever, the hybrid Sian

programme.title: Bloodlands
programme.desc: Tom Brannick enlists the help of Heather Pentland to find out what Jackie Twomey knows about Goliath. Tori Matthews and Tom's daughter Izzy put pressure on Tom to get to the truth. With lives on the line, Tom must unmask Goliath before it's too late. Crime thriller, starring James Nesbitt and Lorcan Cranitch

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v West Ham United at Old Trafford. Spurs prevailed 2-0 over their North London rivals in December's reverse fixture thanks to goals by the deadly duo of Heung-Min Son and Harry Kane, but the Gunners will have been determined for revenge on home soil. United had already defeated the Hammers at home in the FA Cup and also prevailed 3-1 in the league at London Stadium. Plus, Leicester City v Sheffield United and Southampton v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: Driven: The Billy Monger Story
programme.desc: Documentary following 18-year-old Billy Monger as he attempts to get back into motor racing after being involved in a crash in April 2017 that resulted in the amputation of his legs. With the support of the racing community and his hero Lewis Hamilton, Billy will have to change the rules of motorsport and learn to drive again without legs, all while coming to terms with life as a person with a disability

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Married couple Sam and Louise from Aberdeen take on The Wall, hoping to combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Will they get the questions right? And can they walk away with cash to buy a bigger house for when they can start a family? Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon and his team travel to rural Oxfordshire to meet Asiyla and her family and help transform an old sailing boat into unique glamping accommodation.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah showcases a selection of Cotswolds properties to a couple from Essex who are hoping their budget of £550,000 will buy them a new home with three bedrooms, one of which could be used for a home office. The presenter also visits a quarry from which Cotswold stone is used for new builds and conservation projects

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Windermere Jetty Museum in the Lake District, where items assessed include a collection of silver trophies awarded to a pioneering female sailor. There is also a James Bond poster scraped off a wall, a guitar rescued from a skip, a wooden dingy from the film Swallows & Amazons, and a pair of necklaces with surprising values

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: For today's exercises, Mark Wright is joined by Strictly and reality TV star Jamie Laing

programme.title: Bitesize: 11-12 Year Olds
programme.desc: Energy stores in physics, guiding the reader in English, and finding inspiration within ourselves in music

programme.title: Bitesize: 12-13 Year Olds
programme.desc: The science of sound waves and leitmotifs in music

programme.title: Bitesize: 13-14 Year Olds
programme.desc: Magnetic fields in science and writing song lyrics in music

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The family reflect on their time travels, discovering that much of what they experienced in the past, still lives on around our dinner tables today. Polly Russell shows them the delights of a contemporary canned meal, and Brandon and Rochelle sample a Peruvian dinner with co-presenter Giles Coren. Finally the Robshaws share a celebratory meal with Giles and Polly as they realise how many of our historical eating habits are still a treasured part of modern family mealtimes

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Chuko Ojiri hopes a wooden beetle will fly at the auction, while a job lot of broken crockery threatens disaster for Eric Knowles in the week's finale

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Eric Knowles takes on Danny Sebastian at an antiques fair in Nottinghamshire, with the two taking very different approaches to buying and selling

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: In York, the winners of the best detached, best apartment, best semi-detached and best wildcard property are revisited by the judges for a second inspection. The arbiters must make their ultimate decision and crown only one of them the week's Best House in Town

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Claire Rawle and Thomas Plant look out for potential treasures at Chiddingstone Castle in Kent, while presenter Paul Martin visits a hop garden and hears about the thousands of Londoners who descended on farms to pick the hops in times gone by

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mina Anwar, Glenn Moore, Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Shaun Williamson keep their eyes on the prize as Richard Osman reveals who is the week's winner

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: In the Cotswolds, Simon Parfett and his team meet Tammy and Colin. Wanting to spend more time together as a family and create extra income, they decided to buy a horsebox which they could rent out to festival-goers. Alas, things didn't go to plan, so Simon and company come to the rescue

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Reece Parkinson and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Broadcaster and rambler Stuart Maconie walks along the River Hodder in the Forest of Bowland, enjoying the scenery and examining intriguing myths and snippets of history

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team look back at more floral highlights from 2020, including alliums and auriculas, houseplants and creative containers, as well as the scented stars of the summer garden. There's also another chance to see what the Gardeners' World viewers were getting up to in their gardens last year as they share their horticultural adventures

programme.title: The Comedy Vaults: BBC2's Hidden Treasure
programme.desc: As part of the channel's 50th birthday celebrations, this programme delves into the archives for rarely seen moments from some of the greatest names in comedy. Featuring clips from unbroadcast pilots including Miranda Hart's Joke Shop, Stephen Fry's first appearance as QI host and a sitcom starring the band Madness, as well as footage of Spike Milligan, Peter Cook and Dudley Moore, Rik Mayall, Billy Connolly, and Sacha Baron Cohen. Tamsin Greig narrates

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The host chats to award-winning British songstress Laura Mvula, who discusses her favourite performances from the Later archive. Plus, South London four-piece Dry Cleaning make their TV debut. And singer/songwriter Paul Weller performs a track from his new LP, Fat Pop (Volume 1) at his Black Barn studios in Surrey

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Festival of Funny: Shorts
programme.desc: A bold, contemporary collection of the silliest, most distinctive comedy short films. Platforming the next generation of comedy voices, the films showcase the most exciting creative talent from all across the UK and beyond

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Tyrol seeks advice from the imprisoned Baltar after clashing with Admiral Adama over the need for safer working conditions on board the ship, and Seelix becomes involved in the argument after being turned down for flight training

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Starbuck's mental stability causes concern with Admiral Adama and Lee as she struggles to come to terms with her time spent in captivity on New Caprica

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and the team are in rural Wiltshire to help Tom and Jen turn their old family caravan into a unique and profitable short-term rental accommodation

programme.title: Miss World 1970: Beauty Queens and Bedlam
programme.desc: The story of the protest at the contest, when protestors dramatically disrupted the event and kickstarted a feminist revolution in front of an audience of millions. Hannah Berryman's film uses archive, animation and contributions from key players to offer an entertaining and thought-provoking celebration of a night of game-changing anarchy

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Best friends Tom and Alfie attempt to bring the Australian Outback to the West Midlands, complete with a bug-eating competition and an upside-down set

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: A nine-year-old who lives in one of the UK's most famous summer hotspots longs for a Christmas-themed den where she can celebrate her favourite season all year round

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Junior Bake Off winner Reece makes a Mother's Day treat

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Norway to dive with the orca

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall witnesses a serval hunting in South Africa

programme.title: Tinker Bell and the Pirate Fairy (2014)
programme.desc: A rebellious fairy steals the magic pixie dust that grants her people the power of flight and runs away, becoming captain of a band of pirates. Tinker Bell and her friends set off to recover the dust, but succumb to a spell that switches their magical powers. Disney animated adventure, with the voices of Mae Whitman, Christina Hendricks and Tom Hiddleston

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don brings his globe-trotting botanical tour to a close with a visit to South-east Asia, where he hopes to witness a true tropical oasis. Visiting the immaculately presented garden of Jim Thompson House in Bangkok, he discovers an exotic plot with a Western flavour. He also meets former president Lee Kuan Yew, who once stated that Singapore should be 'a city in a garden'

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces animals that build structures and adapt the environment for their own benefit, creating safe places to sleep and raise young or traps to catch prey. The programme examines how the huge dams built by beavers can completely transform the surrounding landscape, the function of the different types of silk spiders are capable of producing, and how leafcutter ants build underground gardens where they farm their own food

programme.title: The Chronicles of Nadiya
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following Great British Bake Off winner Nadiya Hussain as she takes a food-inspired journey and returns to her roots in Bangladesh. In the first edition, she travels to her family village near Sylhet in the north east of the country, where she shares her favourite recipes, including baked fish with green mango chutney and thoosha shinni. For a relative's wedding, she bakes a three-tiered pumpkin celebration cake for the bride, and she also takes the opportunity to reconnect with family she has not seen since her arranged marriage over ten years ago

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Chris Beardshaw makes over Susan and Brian Duthie's new-build garden in Cove, Aberdeenshire, transforming their tiny site from the nightmare of rough builder's rubble

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Mandy and Chris adore their Victorian home in Manchester, but they cannot unlock the potential of their garden. Overlooked on all sides, the couple feel exposed whenever they spend time in it. Providing inspiration for the area are designers Helen Elks-Smith and Pip Probert. Meanwhile, Joel Bird meets Ben and Elaine, a Wiltshire couple who have an unusual outdoor space. Angela Scanlon hosts

programme.title: Up Periscope (1959)
programme.desc: A US Navy frogman is ordered to steal a vital Japanese code book from a Pacific island - but the captain of the submarine entrusted with taking him into enemy waters disapproves of the plan, causing tensions to rise and threatening the mission's success. Second World War adventure, with James Garner, Edmond O'Brien and Alan Hale Jr

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. This time, Stacy meets 31-year-old Duri, who fears she may carry a potentially fatal genetic mutation and wants to test her DNA to see if she could suffer from the same disease as her mother. Elsewhere, a woman who was adopted when she was just six-months-old wants to use DNA technology to finally uncover information about her ancestry

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As they fight to secure a place in the semi-finals, the four remaining designers are challenged to transform two hair salons in Richmond, London, and for the first time in the competition, the designers will see their spaces before creating their visions. Presenter Alan Carr and judge Michelle Ogundehin will then decide who makes it through to the next stage

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: Historian Dominic Sandbrook follows up his 2012 documentary series The 70s by moving on to the following decade. He begins by examining how powerful new forces of choice and consumerism radically reshaped British life - a new culture of consumer-driven populism that propelled Margaret Thatcher to victory. He takes in everything from the popularity of Delia Smith and affordable fashions on the high street, to the subcultures of Britain's youth and the crisis of identity that rocked and splintered the political left

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the music show's evolution, and the dramatic changes that took place in 1980 when a new generation of British pop arrived, leading to the veteran Top of the Pops orchestra being laid off, and the studio audience doubling in size. Featuring Adam Ant, the Human League, OMD, Kevin Rowland, Sally Lindsay, Kelly Marie, Ray Dorset, Johnny Logan, the Vapors, the Piranhas, and Richard Skinner

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Bill Bailey, Jack Carroll and Olga Koch join regular Alan Davies as Sandi Toksvig hosts an extended edition of the quiz with a difference, asking questions on the theme of rogue

programme.title: Billie: In Search of Billie Holiday (2020)
programme.desc: A profile of the American jazz singer, capturing her depths and complexity through interviews conducted by journalist Linda Lipnack Kuehl with those who knew her best. With contributions by Charles Mingus, Tony Bennett, Sylvia Syms and Count Basie, as well as her cousin, schoolfriends, lovers, lawyers, pimps and even the FBI agents who arrested her

programme.title: Whiplash (2014)
programme.desc: A talented young jazz drummer thinks all his dreams have come true when he lands a place at a prestigious music academy. However, the experience turns into a nightmare thanks to an uncompromising tutor who will stop at nothing to make his students realise their potential - including physical and mental abuse. Drama, starring Miles Teller, JK Simmons and Paul Reiser

programme.title: Jazz Divas Gold
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances by acclaimed female jazz artists from shows including Not So Much a Programme - More a Way of Life, Morecambe and Wise, Parkinson and Show of the Week. Featuring Ella Fitzgerald, Peggy Lee, Blossom Dearie, Nina Simone, Cleo Laine, Marion Montgomery, Betty Carter, Sarah Vaughan, Eartha Kitt and Amy Winehouse

programme.title: Clouds of Sils Maria (2014)
programme.desc: A famous actress prepares to star in a new production of the play that made her career in a supporting part, with the lead role now played by a scandalous Hollywood star. She and her assistant head to a cottage in the Swiss mountains to prepare, which leads her to reflect on the harsh truths about how her life has changed. Drama, starring Juliette Binoche, Kristen Stewart and Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the multicultural community of Wednesfield, Wolverhampton, who are hoping to go one better than the silver award they won last year. But they do not have a plan, have a shoestring budget and are relying on locals to donate the plants

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden's balloon travels over the flattest part of Britain as she descends on Norfolk. She helps cut a hole in a hedge at East Ruston Gardens, assists in preparing a tree for felling at Bressingham Gardens, and discovers the human history of the Norfolk Broads

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith visits the Colne Valley Regional Park, a landscape of forest, fields and waterways on the edge of west London. She does battle with an invasive species, gets up close with tiny creatures that show the health of the park's rivers, meets a savvy septuagenarian saving her farm from development, and helps secure a future for one of our most endangered mammals, the water vole. Tom Heap asks whether eco-education should be at the heart of our school system, Adam Henson introduces new boar George II to his sows, and wildlife cameraman Jack Perks gets up close with otters

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team look back at more floral highlights from 2020, including alliums and auriculas, houseplants and creative containers, as well as the scented stars of the summer garden. There's also another chance to see what the Gardeners' World viewers were getting up to in their gardens last year as they share their horticultural adventures

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway is in fine voice when he kicks off his walk in Cardigan, home of the first Eisteddfod. After visiting the castle, lovingly restored by locals, he learns about the dying 'art' of Seine net fishing before climbing the famous mount at Mwnt. He also gets to feed an alpaca and take tea with the Women's Institute at his final destination, Aberporth - where he meets a fellow meteorologist, 98-year-old Joan

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from the programme

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef begins an exploration of the country's food in the states of West Bengal and Tamil Nadu, where the influence of the British Empire on eating habits can still be observed. Rick enjoys the prominence of fish and seafood in the cuisine found in the cities of Kolkata and Chennai, cooking dishes including prawns in coconut milk and his mum's British Raj curry

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Gary Pe help two teams scour an antiques fair at the National Botanic Garden of Wales, while host Charlie Ross shares his passion for cricket

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Brenda Edwards finds out how St Elizabeth's in Stockport makes Mothering Sunday special, and there is a visit to a new Christian community grocery run by a Manchester-based charity

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A selection of film and TV appearances made by the actor, dancer and choreographer, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Singin' in the Rain (1952)
programme.desc: A silent-movie star tries to win the heart of an aspiring actress as the introduction of sound changes the film business for ever. However, his frequent on-screen love interest does not take kindly to this real-life romance. Musical comedy, starring Gene Kelly, Debbie Reynolds, Donald O'Connor, Jean Hagen and Millard Mitchell. Songs include Make 'em Laugh, You Are My Lucky Star and the title number

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Raj Bisram value items brought into Aberystwyth University's Old College, and Paul Martin finds out how the myth of Merlin has been connected to the heart of Wales

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In the Cairngorms, hill farmer Johnny and his son gather in their sheep for pregnancy scanning and mineral dosing on a cold snowy day. Meanwhile in County Down, Northern Ireland, the Rodgers family's cattle are tested by a vet for Bovine TB, and unfortunately find out that six animals are found to be infected, and have to be sent away to slaughter

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels the length of California. He climbs to the top of one of the world's tallest trees, and meets the fire crews tasked with tackling the ever-present danger of wildfires, a growing threat given the state's changing climate and chronic shortage of water. He also meets street doctors providing much-needed medical support to people living in extreme poverty and visits a city on the desert for Americans who have dropped out of the rat race entirely

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: Chris Packham shines a light on some of the species that show how brainpower, ingenuity and clever tactics are just as important as brawn in the daily battle for survival. Spotted hyenas play politics to gain power, and the female with the most friends rules their group, while being a team player can help when it comes to getting a meal - as demonstrated by vampire bats, who share mouthfuls of blood with strangers in the hope of building a bond and receiving the favour in return

programme.title: Brazil with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The seasoned traveller explores the South American country, beginning in the north-east - where Europeans first landed and grew rich on the profits from sugar and tobacco plantations run with slave labour. In Sao Luis, Michael finds out about a ceremony based on a 200-year-old tale before heading to the coastal lagoons of the Lencois Maranhenses National Park. Journeying inland, he gets a glimpse of the fast-disappearing world of old-style cowboys known as vaqueiros, has his fortune read by a Candomble priest and learns to drum with the Olodum cultural collective

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is joined on the topical comedy show by guests Richard Madeley and Maisie Adam, as well as his mum and his hand-picked focus group of 20. Together they take a humorous look at the week's biggest talking points, from front page news to trending topics, helping the nation to navigate their way through these tricky times

programme.title: Beep
programme.desc: Tommy is lying in his hospital bed in a coma and on his wedding anniversary he is visited by his wife Liz and various other members of his dysfunctional family, including his emotionally challenged son Martin and sleep-deprived daughter Hannah. Comedy, starring Siobhan Redmond and Paul Higgins

programme.title: Stephen Merchant: Hello Ladies
programme.desc: The Office co-creator's critically acclaimed 2011 stand-up comedy show, based on his experiences of the rocky road to finding love - a quest in which being taller-than-average doesn't always help

programme.title: Festival of Funny: Shorts
programme.desc: A collection of distinctive comedy shorts from the next generation, showcasing creative talent from all across the UK and beyond

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Claire shares her secret with Gretchen and invites her to be room-mates. Sylar continues to cause chaos in Matt's mind while he and his partner seek evidence against a drug lord. Samuel travels to New York, where he tricks his way into Peter's life, and a deaf filing clerk comes to terms with her new-found abilities. Fantasy drama, starring Jack Coleman, Hayden Panettiere and Milo Ventimiglia

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: While Hiro is trying to stop a colleague from committing suicide, Ando persuades him to tell his sister that he is terminally ill. Tracy struggles to control her powers, and finds she is no longer able to deal with the indignities of her former job. Meanwhile, Nathan's mother helps him regain some of his memories. Drama, starring Masi Oka, Ali Larter and Adrian Pasdar

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: A look at the steeplejack's admiration for ordinary workers. From the labourers and stonemasons who built great medieval castles, to 20th-century coal miners and mill workers, Fred recognises that without hard graft nothing would have been possible

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: A celebration of the later part of the decade's psychedelic music, featuring counter-culture classics by the Who, Pink Floyd, Joe Cocker, the Nice and the Jimi Hendrix Experience

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on October 4, with performances by artists including Twenty 4 Seven featuring Captain Hollywood, Pet Shop Boys, MC Tunes versus 808 State, Hi Tek 3 featuring Ya Kid K, MC Hammer, Technotronic, Bobby Vinton and Maria McKee

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on October 11, with performances by artists including the Sisters of Mercy, the Chimes, the Beautiful South, Neneh Cherry, Monie Love featuring True Image, Cliff Richard, Status Quo, New Kids on the Block, Maria McKee and Depeche Mode

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from

programme.title: Eric Burdon: Rock 'n' Roll - Animal
programme.desc: How pop group the Animals were important standard bearers of the 'British Invasion' of America, behind the Beatles but ahead of the Rolling Stones, the Who and the Kinks. Alongside his passion for original American blues, frontman Eric Burdon got together in the late 1960s with the black LA band WAR - itself a political statement in the Black Panther era - and, inspired by Charlie Parker, John Coltrane and Rahsaan Roland Kirk, expanded his musical spectrum with jazz and funk. This documentary reveals how Burdon's creative output has made an important and profoundly authentic contribution to popular culture

programme.title: Robert Plant: By Myself
programme.desc: The singer discusses his varied life and musical career, including his upbringing in the West Midlands town of Stourbridge, his rise to stardom as the singer of influential rock band Led Zeppelin and his solo projects. The programme also reflects on his collaboration with country singer Alison Krauss, with whom he made the Grammy-winning album Raising Sand, and his reunion with virtuoso guitarist Jimmy Page

programme.title: Southern Rock at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by classic Southern rock acts on shows including The Old Grey Whistle Test, In Concert and Wogan. Featuring Lynyrd Skynyrd, the Charlie Daniels Band, the Ozark Mountain Daredevils, Delaney & Bonnie with Eric Clapton, Dickey Betts, formerly of the Allman Brothers Band, the Marshall Tucker Band, Black Oak Arkansas, Greg Allman with Cher, and Edgar Winter

programme.title: Meat Loaf: In and Out of Hell
programme.desc: A profile of the rock star's life and career, reflecting on the music that propelled him to fame and charting his journey from an overweight, bullied child to his larger-than-life persona and icon status. Featuring an interview and performance by the singer, as well as backstage footage from a Las Vegas concert. The film also revisits the Dallas of Meat Loaf's early years and includes insights from high school friends who reveal where the unusual moniker really came from

programme.title: Eric Burdon: Rock 'n' Roll - Animal
programme.desc: How pop group the Animals were important standard bearers of the 'British Invasion' of America, behind the Beatles but ahead of the Rolling Stones, the Who and the Kinks. Alongside his passion for original American blues, frontman Eric Burdon got together in the late 1960s with the black LA band WAR - itself a political statement in the Black Panther era - and, inspired by Charlie Parker, John Coltrane and Rahsaan Roland Kirk, expanded his musical spectrum with jazz and funk. This documentary reveals how Burdon's creative output has made an important and profoundly authentic contribution to popular culture

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: A look at the steeplejack's admiration for ordinary workers. From the labourers and stonemasons who built great medieval castles, to 20th-century coal miners and mill workers, Fred recognises that without hard graft nothing would have been possible

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: Documentary following the fortunes of humans and animals over the course of a year in Alaska, which is by far the biggest US state and still one of the wildest places on Earth with deep forests and vast mountain ranges. The arrival of spring sees Alaska go through a huge transformation as water, light and warmth return, with animals emerging from hibernation and rushing to cash in on the riches of the season. Dougray Scott narrates

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose discovers the truth behind the first circumnavigation of the world. More than 500 years ago, Ferdinand Magellan set out to find the riches of the Spice Islands, but contrary to popular perception, he failed to reach them. A dramatic sequence of events followed, involving mutiny and murder, as his scurvy-ridden crew continued the journey without him

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Believing that Kalle is in danger, Mikko traces his phone and rushes to a neighbouring island where he finds and accosts Leo. Kalle, who has overheard his uncle's confession, tells Mikko who really killed Tommi. In Finnish

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Ellu admits to Tytti that he doesn't think that her mother likes him as Daniel drives Anna to their surprise weekend away. In Finnish

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver tells the story of how the age of the Vikings came to an end, using archaeological evidence to explore the lives of the first Danish kings, the spread of Christianity in Scandinavia, and the voyages of discovery undertaken by Norse sailors in the Atlantic. He also profiles Canute, the former king of England, and reveals why the reputation he has accrued over the past millennium does a disservice to his diplomatic feats

programme.title: Glastonbury 2014
programme.desc: The country singer's complete set on the Pyramid stage at Worthy Farm in the now-traditional legends spot on Sunday afternoon, featuring Jolene, Coat of Many Colours, 9 to 5, Islands in the Stream and I Will Always Love You

programme.title: Country Kings at the BBC
programme.desc: A chronological compilation of performances from the BBC archive by male country singers, ranging from the Everly Brothers and Jerry Lee Lewis to Garth Brooks and Willie Nelson. programme.title: The Plank (1967)
programme.desc: Two clumsy workmen have the job of transporting a plank through a London suburb to a building site. Slapstick comedy directed by Eric Sykes, who also stars alongside Tommy Cooper

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.desc: The Liverpool-born actress reminisces about her career, including her successes with director and ex-husband Mike Leigh, with whom she created the monstrous Beverly in 1970s play Abigail's Party. She focuses on her early screen appearances, and reveals what influenced her choice of roles. With clips from Gavin & Stacey, Newshounds, Nuts in May and Frost Weekly

programme.title: Twin Sisters: A World Apart
programme.desc: The story of identical twin girls Alexandra and Mia, who were adopted separately from China, with one growing up in California, and the other in Sweden. A chance sighting at the orphanage prompted the adoptive parents to suspect the girls were related, and a DNA test proved their theory. The film follows the children as they meet in Norway aged eight years old, and captures the similarities between the pair, despite having grown up thousands of miles apart

programme.title: I Got Life! (2017)
programme.desc: A woman, separated from her husband, loses her job and then finds out she is going to be a grandmother. However, her life takes an unexpected turn when she crosses paths with the great love of her youth. Drama, starring Agnes Jaoui and Thibault de Montalembert. In French

programme.title: Opera Mums with Bryony Kimmings
programme.desc: Inspired by the lives of single mums and informed by opera experts at the English National Opera, performance artist Bryony Kimmings writes and directs her own operetta. The demands of the opera industry are turned on their head by Kimmings' insistence that real life is just as deserving of high art as the stories of the rich and powerful

programme.desc: Lauren Layfield hosts as two teams fight it out to dodge detention and put the cool back into school. Trying to win Lauren’s shiny gold stars are team captains Olivia and Nayan. They are joined by Blue Peter’s Lyndsey Russell and actress Dúaa Karim plus comedians Sam Fletcher and Helen Bauer. They all battle it out in a series of hilarious rounds guaranteed to make you laugh! Rounds include Say What and Stick to the Point, the word association game where teams are put on the spot by Lauren and her stick of pointiness, and everyone’s favourite, Pie the Supply. This week they are all pretending to be home economics teachers, but only one of them is telling the truth. So watch someone get pied in the face before the final round, Gnomework, where teams must dash across gunge infested fields to grab some homework to avoid getting detention.

programme.title: Tom and Jerry & The Lost Dragon (2014)
Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse's over-reliance on gadgets prompts Colonel K to send his top agent on a tech-free nature retreat. Unbeknownst to Danger Mouse, Greenback has the same plans for his daughter, Delilah, whose social media obsession has also spiralled out of control. Back at HQ Penfold is tasked with protecting the entire world from the threat of super-villainy. Stripped off his gadgets, DM struggles to adapt to life in the wilderness and, when a talking pinecone called Conni begins to offer him survival tips, he begins to question his own sanity. Soon enough, Delilah catches wind of DM's presence and she plots to destroy him as a means of getting back into her Dad's good books. With the voices of Alexander Armstrong and Kevin Eldon

programme.title: Danger Mouse

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: There's relief all around when Penfold's pilfering of Squawk's gadgets and clandestine late night trips to shady warehouses turns out to preparation for his taking part in 'bot battles' - a fight club for homemade robots. Penfold hopes to enter his own creation, Geoff, into the competition but is sidelined by an overeager Danger Mouse and his own robot, the CRN 3000! When the CRN 3000 obliterates all challengers, DM's call for a worthy opponent is answered by Megahurtz. But Megahurtz may have a secret android army agenda...

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Safari World is opening the Arctic Dome to visitors and Mr Hammond has chosen animal costumes for Andy and Jen to wear while they show people around. Jen informs Mr Hammond and Andy that there is trouble in the Arctic Dome with the walrus’s not having enough space due to a recent baby boom. Mr Hammond is worried that the walrus pups will get squished and can’t work out why there is not enough space. Andy suggests Mr Hammond goes and entertains the visitors while he and Jen work out what’s wrong. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Donning his adventure gear, Andy takes to the skies in his safari-mobile, jetting off to the Arctic. Jen picks up some activity not far from the safari-mobile, so she suggests that’s a good place to start. Andy discovers that Jen

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle and Mrs Hobbs are poorly. Waffle has to rest while the Brooklyn-Bells do chores for Mrs Hobbs to help her get better. Jess brings home a surprise from work

programme.title: Molly and Mack

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. There has been a big tropical storm at Safari World and Andy has made sure all the animals are safe and well. The storm has damaged the electricity network. Mr Hammond explains that he has been checking on all the bird’s nests, but there is one nest he is not able to find: the nest of a storm petrel, which is a type of sea bird. Mr Hammond is worried that their nest may have blown away. Andy decides to call Jen to check the park’s database to find out where they nest. Unfortunately, due to the power outages, the database is not working! It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Donning his adventure gear, Andy takes to the skies in his safari-mobile, jetting off to the Galapagos Islands. Andy is unable to get hold of Jen, but luckily the Galapagos Islands are one

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace shows off three machines that all move because of propellers. She flies a seaplane, shows off unusual hydrocopter and goes for a fast ride in a raceboat

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Charlie, Etti and their families as they discover the world around them at three months old and learn what makes them both smile. We join Etti, who is having a fun day out with her family at some local gardens. They see lots of different flowers and animals including a big rabbit statue! But will these make Etti and her sister, Grace, smile? Meanwhile, Charlie and his family are out visiting a zoo where they see lots of different birds. Mum does an impression that makes Charlie smile, so his family use an instant camera to take some lovely family photos - but what will they do with them? After a quick stop at the local park, Etti and Grace decide to stop at a friend’s house to play peek-a-boo together. Grace creates her own happy faces with cut-out features. Finally we see what Charlie and his family were taking photos for - a photo album! What a fun and special way to look back on their family day out.

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie explores some favourite foods. She uses a special slow motion camera to find out why popcorn pops. An animation shows what popcorn is made of and how a water droplet hidden inside each kernel of corn expands as it is cooked, making it pop. Next, Maddie visits a pasta factory to see how flour is mixed with water and turned into pasta dough and how special cutters are used to create different pasta shapes

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Dina Lady and The Lose Pirate join team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt for funny clips and questions. Round 1 is a simple clip game with a question for each team. Dina Lady and Gail are up first with a clip featuring Rapids Johnson unwittingly buying ice cream from a cheeky bear, but can the team remember what extra toppings were on Rapids' ice cream? Keith and The Lost Pirate are up next with a clip featuring Wild Wes packing up his mule, but can the team remember the final thing Wes placed on top of the pile? When Round 1 is over, all the talk of ice creams earlier Dina decides to make some delicious ice creams for everybody using her new ice cream machine. Gail thinks it looks very complicated, and it ends in a mess! Round 2 is next, and it’s time for Nana’s Panto Showdown. This time, Nana’s spiders have made themselves a giant web. The teams each guess how many flies they think the spiders will catch in their sticky net. Finally, it’s time for the buzzer round, and Dina decides

programme.desc: The school's teacher is retiring, which worries Katie, on top of news that her mum and dad are having another baby

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles returns with the programme in which three artists are challenged to create pieces for people they have never me, beginning by travelling to Shropshire to meet a buyer who wants to mark her recent return to her roots following a health scare

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Health professionals Hayley and Sunni compete. The pair met on social media over their shared love of a plant-based diet, so in an all-vegan show contest are keen to take the ingredients they cook with all the time and elevate them to new heights. Paired up with chef Romy Gill, Hayley is keen to rustle up some mouth-watering vegan treats in the Green Pepper kitchen. In the Red Tomato kitchen, Sunni is paired with chef Jeremy Pang, who brings his own flair and style to Sunni's usual ingredients. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers compete to buy items including an Edwardian shoe closet, a bronze racehorse, a 17th-century writing desk and Concorde service medals. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
